title: Event-triggered stale documentation detection
description: Docs rot silently. Build an event-triggered staleness detector: when a directive completes, scan docs that reference modified files/systems and flag potentially stale content. This is NOT a new agent or skill — it's a post-directive hook (Step 6 addition) that outputs 'Potentially Stale Docs' in the digest. Lightweight: grep + timestamp check, not a knowledge graph.

in: Post-directive hook: scan .context/ docs that reference files modified in this directive
Staleness heuristic: doc references files changed in directive but doc hasn't been updated
Output: 'Potentially Stale Docs' section in digest with file paths and what changed
Lightweight: grep + git log timestamps, no embeddings or semantic analysis
out: Automatic doc updates (only detection, not fixing)
Scheduled runs (only triggered by directive completion)

dod.criterion: After a directive modifies code, the digest lists docs that reference those files but weren't updated
Zero false positives on docs that WERE updated in the same directive
Detection runs in <5 seconds (grep + timestamp, not expensive analysis)

checklist: Run directive that modifies a system file — verify related docs appear in Potentially Stale section
Run directive that modifies a system file AND updates its docs — verify no false positive
Verify detection adds <5s to directive completion time

reviewers.domain: architecture — detection accuracy, performance, no over-engineering

tasks.title: Design staleness detection heuristic — what counts as 'references', what counts as 'stale'
Build detection script — grep .context/ for file references, compare with git log
Integrate into pipeline Step 6 — add 'Potentially Stale Docs' to digest template
Test with directive that touches system files — verify detection and no false positives
